matches.match_rationale: The core pain is inability to articulate the total compensation story — tuition waivers, benefits, and mission value — in a compelling way during offer negotiation. Text generation can produce tailored offer narratives that translate opaque benefit structures into concrete, personalized financial comparisons. Multi-campus variability in HQ vs. branch compensation packages can each have purpose-built messaging templates generated on demand.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A tool that takes candidate profile inputs (role, campus, family size, prior salary) and generates a personalized total-compensation narrative letter showing tuition waiver value, retirement contributions, and comparable take-home vs. corporate/international school alternatives — ready for the recruiter to send or present.

matches.match_rationale: Multi-campus compensation variability creates confusion because leaders lack a shared quantitative model of total compensation equivalence across sites. A regression model trained on accepted vs. rejected offers, role type, campus, and benefit mix can estimate the effective total compensation value of each package — giving recruiters a defensible number to anchor negotiations. This directly addresses the 15-25% offer rejection signal by identifying which compensation components swing candidate decisions.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compensation equivalence model that inputs role, campus, and benefit bundle and outputs an estimated total-comp dollar figure comparable to market benchmarks, enabling recruiters to say 'this package is equivalent to $X at a corporate employer' with data backing.

matches.match_rationale: Recruiters and hiring managers currently lack fast access to accurate, up-to-date compensation policy details across campuses — including tuition waiver tiers, eligibility windows, and benefit comparisons — leading to inconsistent messaging. A RAG system over internal HR policy documents, benefit plan summaries, and campus-specific compensation guides enables recruiters to get precise, sourced answers during live offer conversations rather than guessing or delaying. This directly solves the multi-campus variability confusion problem at the point of negotiation.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An internal recruiter assistant that answers questions like 'What is the tuition waiver value for a part-time staff member at the downtown campus?' by retrieving the exact policy and generating a clear, plain-language answer — deployable as a Slack bot or embedded HR portal widget.

matches.match_rationale: Candidates themselves are often confused about how tuition waivers and benefit structures compare to market — and recruiters cannot always be present to clarify. A conversational agent embedded in the candidate portal or reachable via chat could walk candidates through a total-compensation comparison interactively, answering follow-up questions about eligibility, dependents coverage, and campus-specific nuances that a static FAQ cannot address. This reduces recruiter load while improving candidate clarity during the critical post-offer window.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A candidate-facing benefits FAQ chatbot that guides prospective hires through a personalized total-compensation breakdown — 'How does the tuition waiver work if my child enrolls?' — reducing offer rejection driven by benefit misunderstanding without requiring recruiter time.
